name: Dapansutrile showed early HFrEF safety with exploratory functional signals
summary: Direct NLRP3 inhibition has human safety and pharmacodynamic footholds, but remains early for aging-relevant outcomes.
statement: In a 30-person phase 1B dose-escalation trial in stable HFrEF, 14 days of dapansutrile was reported as safe and well tolerated, with within-cohort exploratory improvements in LVEF and exercise time at the 2000 mg dose.
population_or_model: Stable NYHA II-III systolic heart failure patients
time_horizon: Up to 14 days of treatment with day 28 follow-up
quantitative_note: No serious adverse events occurred; in the 2000 mg cohort, LVEF increased from 31.5% to 36.5% and exercise time from 570 to 616 seconds within cohort.
caveats: The trial was small, single-center, and designed primarily for safety.
Placebo participants were used as a decoy and not for statistical comparisons.
The findings are disease-specific and short-term, not evidence of healthy-aging benefit.
